_comment: Phraseology-hint matrix. First matching rule wins. Missing fields match anything ('any' is also accepted explicitly). 'phase' and 'frequency_type' may be a single name or an array. 'tower_only' and 'post_landing' are booleans (missing = any). The READBACK override (atc_state_machine::is_readback_pending) and the cold-and-dark override (start_mode + com_radio_powered) are applied AFTER this lookup -- rules here describe only the stationary intent menu. EN profile; intent keys are language-neutral and identical to the de/ bundle.

_schema.intents: Array of intent keys; empty array = no hints (UI shows tune hint)

rules.comment: IDLE after landing, on Ground frequency during roll-out / taxi to apron: only taxi-to-apron makes sense.

Tower-only field after landing on Tower frequency: the same controller handles taxi. Offer both RUNWAY_VACATED (still rolling out) and REQUEST_TAXI_PARKING (after clearing the runway). The taxi_controller variable renders 'Tower' in the phraseology; the frequency filter is bypassed by the tower_only exception in the UI defense layer.
2-tier field after landing on Tower frequency: pilot owes RUNWAY_VACATED before switching back to Ground.
IDLE cold start at the apron: pilot must establish contact with Ground.
Tower-only field, IDLE at the apron on Tower frequency: the initial call is a taxi request to Tower (collapse-to-tower happens via intent_category in the UI).
IDLE at the apron on Tower frequency at a 2-tier field: empty hint list so the UI shows 'Tune Ground first'.
IDLE at the apron, no usable frequency tuned: empty list, UI shows tune hint.

IDLE airborne near a field on Tower (lost contact / fresh approach / cross-country inbound): recovery via INITIAL_CALL_INBOUND. CRUISE covers the en-route inbound after airport change (symmetric to the AFIS rule and to flight_rules.json INITIAL_CALL_INBOUND.allowed_phases).
GROUND_CONTACT: pilot has greeted Ground, waiting for taxi clearance.
GROUND_CONTACT after landing: REQUEST_TAXI_PARKING after greeting Ground for taxi to apron.
TAXI_CLEARED: pilot taxiing per clearance. The only legitimate prompt is a readback (handled by override). Empty list keeps the panel quiet.
TOWER_CONTACT at the holding point: pilot is ready for departure. Offer both pattern and cross-country variants.
TOWER_CONTACT inbound from approach (rare but possible): REPORT_POSITION variants.
DEPARTURE_CLEARED on the ground / take-off roll: silent, wait for auto-correction. Applies to pattern and XC departures.
DEPARTURE_CLEARED airborne (cross-country): pilot can request a frequency change or sign off.
PATTERN_ENTRY in the pattern: position reports + landing variants.
PATTERN_ENTRY on final: report final + go-around.
LANDING_CLEARED airborne: pilot waits for readback (override) or needs GO_AROUND.
LANDING_CLEARED on roll-out: pilot owes RUNWAY_VACATED.
TOUCH_AND_GO_CLEARED in the pattern: same flow as PATTERN_ENTRY plus go-around option.
EN_ROUTE near a tower-controlled field: switch to the inbound call.
EN_ROUTE with no relevant frequency: empty -> UI shows tune hint.
Uncontrolled IDLE at the apron: self-announce, optional radio check.
Uncontrolled IDLE airborne near an uncontrolled field: announce inbound on the traffic frequency.
Uncontrolled UNICOM_ACTIVE: self-announce across the whole phase.
AFIS/Info or Radio unit on the ground: initial call to the Info/Radio unit, optional radio check, departure self-announce. Advisory, no clearances.
AFIS/Info or Radio unit airborne: announce inbound, position reports, sign-off.
Final fallback: empty list. UI shows a context-dependent tune hint.
